sql-formatter.title: SQL Formatter
sql-formatter.description: Beautify and format complex SQL queries.
sql-formatter.article: SQL (Structured Query Language) is used to communicate with databases. Our formatter beautifies raw, minified, or messy SQL queries into readable, well-indented formats supporting multiple SQL dialects like MySQL, PostgreSQL, and T-SQL.

faqs.q0: Does formatting change my query logic?
faqs.a0: No, the formatter only alters whitespace and capitalization (if enabled) without modifying the underlying query logic.
faqs.a1: We support Standard SQL, MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQLite, T-SQL (SQL Server), and MariaDB.

custom.tip: Choose the correct dialect to ensure proper formatting of specialized syntax like backticks or schema prefixes.

html-previewer.title: HTML Previewer
html-previewer.description: Render and preview HTML code snippets live.
html-previewer.layoutDesc: The preview runs in a sandboxed iframe for security. External scripts are disabled except for explicitly enabled libraries.
html-previewer.article: The HTML Previewer allows you to write and test HTML and CSS in real-time. It provides a sandboxed environment to visualize your code changes instantly, supporting modern features like Tailwind CSS via CDN.

faqs.a0: Yes, it uses an iframe with the 'sandbox' attribute to prevent malicious script execution.
faqs.q1: Can I use external CSS frameworks?
faqs.a1: Yes! You can toggle 'Include Tailwind' to automatically load Tailwind CSS via CDN.

html-entity-encoder.title: HTML Entity Encoder
html-entity-encoder.description: Encode and decode HTML entities securely. Supports named and numeric entities.
html-entity-encoder.article: HTML entities are used to display reserved characters in HTML that would otherwise be interpreted as code. For example, < becomes < and > becomes >. This encoding is crucial for preventing XSS (Cross-Site Scripting) attacks when displaying user-generated content. This tool supports both named entities (&) and numeric entities (&).

faqs.q0: Why should I encode HTML entities?
faqs.a0: Encoding HTML entities prevents browsers from interpreting special characters as HTML tags or scripts, which is essential for security and preventing XSS attacks when displaying user input.
faqs.q1: What's the difference between named and numeric entities?
faqs.a1: Named entities like < are easier to read, while numeric entities like < work for any Unicode character. Named entities are limited to predefined characters, but numeric entities can represent any character.

html-entity-encoder.html_entity_enc: HTML entity encoding replaces characters like < and > with safe sequences to prevent XSS attacks.

punycode-converter.title: Punycode Converter
punycode-converter.description: Convert Internationalized Domain Names (IDN) to Punycode.
punycode-converter.article: Punycode is a encoding syntax used to represent Unicode characters in domain names using only ASCII characters. Since DNS (Domain Name System) traditionally only supports ASCII, Punycode allows internationalized domain names (IDN) with characters from languages like Arabic, Chinese, Cyrillic, and emoji. Punycode domains start with 'xn--' prefix. For example, 'münchen.de' becomes 'xn--mnchen-3ya.de'.

faqs.q0: Why do I see 'xn--' in some domains?
faqs.a0: The 'xn--' prefix indicates a Punycode-encoded domain name containing non-ASCII characters. It's how browsers and DNS servers handle internationalized domain names while maintaining compatibility with ASCII-only systems.
faqs.a1: Punycode itself is safe, but be cautious of homograph attacks where similar-looking characters from different alphabets (like Cyrillic 'а' vs Latin 'a') are used to create deceptive domain names that look legitimate.

punycode-converter.info: Punycode converts Unicode characters into an ASCII-compatible format required for internationalized domain names (IDNs).

html-minifier.title: HTML Minifier
html-minifier.description: Compress HTML by removing whitespace and comments.
html-minifier.article: HTML minification removes unnecessary characters from HTML code without changing its functionality. This includes whitespace, line breaks, comments, and optional tags. Minified HTML reduces file size, leading to faster page load times and reduced bandwidth usage. This is especially important for production websites where every kilobyte counts for performance and SEO.

faqs.q0: How much size reduction can I expect?
faqs.a0: Typical HTML files can be reduced by 20-40% depending on formatting style. Files with extensive comments and indentation see greater reductions.
faqs.a1: No, minification only removes unnecessary whitespace and comments. The HTML structure and functionality remain intact. However, always test minified code before deploying to production.

html-beautifier.title: HTML Beautifier
html-beautifier.description: Format and beautify HTML with proper indentation.
html-beautifier.article: HTML beautification (also called formatting or pretty-printing) adds proper indentation, line breaks, and spacing to make HTML code more readable and maintainable. Well-formatted code is easier to debug, understand, and collaborate on. This tool automatically organizes your HTML with consistent indentation and structure.

faqs.q0: What indent size should I use?
faqs.a0: 2 or 4 spaces are most common. 2 spaces is popular in modern web development for its compactness, while 4 spaces offers better visual hierarchy. Choose based on your team's style guide.
faqs.a1: This tool focuses on formatting, not validation. It will beautify the HTML you provide, but won't fix structural errors like unclosed tags or invalid nesting.

html-to-markdown.title: HTML to Markdown
html-to-markdown.description: Convert HTML to clean Markdown syntax.
html-to-markdown.article: Converting HTML to Markdown is useful when migrating content from web pages to Markdown-based systems like GitHub, documentation sites, or static site generators. This tool intelligently converts HTML elements to their Markdown equivalents while preserving semantic meaning, links, images, and formatting. The output is clean, readable Markdown that's easy to edit.

faqs.a0: Common elements like headings, paragraphs, links, images, lists, bold, italic, and code blocks are fully supported. Complex elements like tables and forms may have limited support depending on Markdown flavor.
faqs.q1: What happens to CSS and JavaScript?
faqs.a1: Markdown doesn't support CSS or JavaScript. These elements are typically stripped out during conversion, keeping only the semantic content and structure.

markdown-to-html.title: Markdown to HTML
markdown-to-html.description: Convert Markdown to raw HTML output.
markdown-to-html.article: Markdown to HTML conversion transforms lightweight Markdown syntax into standard HTML markup. This is essential for rendering Markdown content on websites, blogs, and documentation platforms. The tool supports standard Markdown syntax including headings, lists, links, images, code blocks, and emphasis, producing clean, semantic HTML ready for use.

faqs.a0: This tool supports CommonMark standard Markdown plus GitHub Flavored Markdown (GFM) extensions including tables, task lists, strikethrough, and syntax highlighting for code blocks.
faqs.q1: Is the HTML safe to use?
faqs.a1: The generated HTML is clean and semantic. However, if you're accepting user-generated Markdown, always sanitize the output to prevent XSS attacks before displaying it on your website.

html-to-jsx.title: HTML to JSX
html-to-jsx.description: Convert HTML to React JSX with proper syntax.
html-to-jsx.article: JSX (JavaScript XML) is a syntax extension for JavaScript used in React. Converting HTML to JSX requires several transformations: 'class' becomes 'className', inline styles become objects, self-closing tags must be properly closed, and attribute names follow camelCase convention. This tool automates these conversions, making it easy to integrate HTML templates into React components.

faqs.q0: What are the main differences between HTML and JSX?
faqs.a0: Key differences: 'class' → 'className', 'for' → 'htmlFor', inline styles use objects, all tags must be closed, attribute names are camelCase (onClick, onChange), and comments use {/* */} syntax.
faqs.q1: Can I paste entire HTML pages?
faqs.a1: Yes, but JSX components typically represent smaller UI pieces. You may need to break large HTML into multiple components. Also, remove <!DOCTYPE> and <html> tags as these aren't used in React components.

meta-tag-generator.title: Meta Tag Generator
meta-tag-generator.description: Generate SEO, Open Graph, and Twitter card meta tags for your website.
meta-tag-generator.article: Meta tags provide metadata about your HTML document. They are used by search engines to index your site and by social media platforms to display rich previews.

faqs.a0: Open Graph tags are meta tags that allow you to control how your content appears when shared on social media platforms like Facebook and LinkedIn.
faqs.q1: Where do I place these tags?
faqs.a1: These tags should be placed inside the <head> section of your HTML document.

meta-tag-generator.author: Author

favicon-generator.title: Favicon Generator
favicon-generator.description: Generate standard favicon HTML tags and preview how they look.
favicon-generator.article: A favicon is a small icon displayed in browser tabs and bookmarks. This tool helps you generate the necessary HTML code to include favicons in your web project.

faqs.q0: What is the standard size for a favicon?
faqs.a0: The standard sizes are 16x16, 32x32, and 48x48 pixels. Modern browsers also use larger icons for mobile bookmarks.
faqs.q1: How do I use the generated code?
faqs.a1: Copy the generated <link> tags and paste them into the <head> section of your HTML.

robots-txt-generator.title: robots.txt Generator
robots-txt-generator.description: Create a robots.txt file to guide search engine crawlers.
robots-txt-generator.article: A robots.txt file tells search engine crawlers which pages or sections of your site should not be processed or scanned.

faqs.a0: The User-agent field specifies which crawler the rule applies to. An asterisk (*) applies the rule to all crawlers.
faqs.a1: No, robots.txt is a request, not a command. While reputable crawlers follow it, malicious ones may ignore it.

sitemap-generator.title: Sitemap Generator
sitemap-generator.description: Generate an XML sitemap to help search engines discover your pages.
sitemap-generator.article: An XML sitemap lists a website's important pages, making sure search engines can find and crawl them all.

faqs.q0: Why do I need a sitemap?
faqs.a0: Sitemaps help search engines find pages that might not be discoverable through normal crawling, especially on large or new websites.
faqs.q1: How many URLs can a sitemap have?
faqs.a1: A single sitemap file can contain up to 50,000 URLs and must not exceed 50MB.

htaccess-generator.title: .htaccess Generator
htaccess-generator.description: Generate common Apache .htaccess rules for redirects and security.
htaccess-generator.article: .htaccess is a configuration file used by Apache web servers to control various aspects of website behavior, including redirects and access control.

faqs.a0: It enables the runtime rewriting engine which is required for most redirection and URL beautification rules.
faqs.a1: No, Nginx does not use .htaccess files. It requires configuration changes in its main server block files.

htaccess-generator.apache_warning: Apache only! Not compatible with Nginx.

iframe-generator.title: iframe Generator
iframe-generator.description: Easily create embeddable iframe tags with custom attributes.
iframe-generator.article: An iframe (Inline Frame) is used to embed another document within the current HTML document.

faqs.a0: The sandbox attribute enables an extra set of restrictions for the content in the iframe to improve security.
faqs.q1: Can I make an iframe responsive?
faqs.a1: Yes, by using CSS (e.g., width: 100% and a container with a specific aspect ratio).

html-table-generator.title: HTML Table Generator
html-table-generator.description: Visually build HTML tables and generate clean code.
html-table-generator.article: HTML tables allow web developers to arrange data into rows and columns. This tool helps you build them without writing manual tags.

faqs.a0: Tables should be used for tabular data, not for page layout.
faqs.a1: It is a CSS property that determines whether table borders are separated or collapsed into a single border.

og-previewer.title: Open Graph Previewer
og-previewer.description: Preview how your website looks on social media (Facebook, Twitter, etc.).
og-previewer.article: Open Graph meta tags are snippets of code that control how URLs are displayed when shared on social media. This tool helps you visualize that display.

faqs.q0: What is the most important OG tag?
faqs.a0: The 'og:title' and 'og:image' are usually the most critical for engagement.
faqs.a1: For privacy, this tool processes meta tags you paste directly rather than fetching external URLs.

og-previewer.preview_desc: This is a preview description of the page content as it will appear on social platforms.
og-previewer.sidebar_desc: OG tags ensure your content looks great when shared, increasing click-through rates.

link-extractor.title: Link Extractor
link-extractor.description: Extract all URLs and links from any HTML code or text.
link-extractor.article: The Link Extractor scans your input for anything that looks like a URL or an <a> tag and lists them for easy copying.

faqs.q0: Can I extract links from a website?
faqs.a0: You can paste the source code of any page into this tool to extract its links.
faqs.q1: What types of links are extracted?
faqs.a1: Both absolute (http://...) and relative (/page) links are identified.

link-extractor.no_links: No links found in the input.
link-extractor.sidebar_desc: This tool extracts both raw URLs and links from HTML anchor tags. It identifies absolute, relative, and internal hash links.

http-status-reference.title: HTTP Status Reference
http-status-reference.description: A comprehensive guide to HTTP response status codes and their meanings.
http-status-reference.article: HTTP response status codes indicate whether a specific HTTP request has been successfully completed. They are grouped into five classes.

faqs.a0: 4xx codes indicate client-side errors (e.g., 404 Not Found).
faqs.a1: 5xx codes indicate server-side errors (e.g., 500 Internal Server Error).

http-status-reference.desc_2xx: Success - Request was received and accepted.
http-status-reference.desc_4xx: Client Error - Request contains bad syntax.
http-status-reference.desc_5xx: Server Error - Server failed to fulfill request.
http-status-reference.code_100_desc: The server has received the request headers and the client should proceed to send the request body.
http-status-reference.code_101_desc: The requester has asked the server to switch protocols.
http-status-reference.code_200_desc: Standard response for successful HTTP requests.
http-status-reference.code_201_desc: The request has been fulfilled, resulting in the creation of a new resource.
http-status-reference.code_204_desc: The server successfully processed the request and is not returning any content.
http-status-reference.code_301_desc: This and all future requests should be directed to the given URI.
http-status-reference.code_302_desc: The resource was found, but at a different URI.
http-status-reference.code_304_desc: Indicates that the resource has not been modified since the version specified by the request headers.
http-status-reference.code_400_desc: The server cannot or will not process the request due to an apparent client error.
http-status-reference.code_401_desc: Specifically for use when authentication is required and has failed or has not yet been provided.
http-status-reference.code_403_desc: The server understood the request, but is refusing action.
http-status-reference.code_404_desc: The requested resource could not be found but may be available in the future.
http-status-reference.code_405_desc: A request method is not supported for the requested resource.
http-status-reference.code_422_desc: The request was well-formed but was unable to be followed due to semantic errors.
http-status-reference.code_426_desc: The client should switch to a different protocol such as TLS/1.0.
http-status-reference.code_429_desc: The user has sent too many requests in a given amount of time.
http-status-reference.code_451_desc: The server is denying access to the resource as a consequence of a legal demand.
http-status-reference.code_500_desc: A generic error message, given when an unexpected condition was encountered.
http-status-reference.code_501_desc: The server either does not recognize the request method, or it lacks the ability to fulfil the request.
http-status-reference.code_502_desc: The server was acting as a gateway or proxy and received an invalid response.
http-status-reference.code_503_desc: The server cannot handle the request (because it is overloaded or down for maintenance).
http-status-reference.code_504_desc: The server was acting as a gateway or proxy and did not receive a timely response.
http-status-reference.code_505_desc: The server does not support the HTTP protocol version used in the request.

mime-type-finder.title: MIME Type Finder
mime-type-finder.description: Look up MIME types by file extension or vice versa.
mime-type-finder.article: A MIME type (now called Media Type) is a standard that indicates the nature and format of a document, file, or assortment of bytes.

faqs.a0: Multipurpose Internet Mail Extensions.
faqs.a1: Browsers use MIME types to determine how to process a file (e.g., display as image or download).

mime-type-finder.sidebar_desc: MIME types are sent by the server in the Content-Type header to tell the browser how to handle the file.
mime-type-finder.no_results: No MIME types found for your search.

cookie-parser.title: Cookie String Parser
cookie-parser.description: Parse complex Cookie headers into readable key-value pairs.
cookie-parser.article: HTTP cookies are small blocks of data created by a web server while a user is browsing a website. This tool decodes the structured string into a readable format.

faqs.a0: It allows servers to assert that a cookie ought not to be sent along with cross-site requests.
faqs.a1: The 'Secure' and 'HttpOnly' flags help protect cookies from being accessed by unauthorized scripts or sent over insecure connections.

cookie-parser.placeholder: name=value; Domain=example.com; Path=/; Expires=Wed, 09 Jun 2021 10:18:14 GMT
cookie-parser.sidebar_desc: The first pair is the actual cookie data. Subsequent pairs are attributes that control scope and security.

user-agent-parser.title: User Agent Parser
user-agent-parser.description: Analyze browser User Agent strings to identify OS, browser, and device.
user-agent-parser.article: The User-Agent request header is a characteristic string that lets servers and network peers identify the application, operating system, vendor, and/or version of the requesting user agent.

faqs.q0: Why do user agents look so complicated?
faqs.a0: They often include legacy strings for backward compatibility with older servers.
faqs.a1: Yes, many browsers and tools allow 'User Agent Spoofing' to appear as a different device.

user-agent-parser.sidebar_desc: Modern User Agent strings are designed to be somewhat compatible with older parsers, which is why most start with 'Mozilla/5.0'.

url-parser.title: URL Parser
url-parser.description: Break down a URL into its components: protocol, host, path, and parameters.
url-parser.article: A Uniform Resource Locator (URL) is a reference to a web resource. This tool decomposes it into its constituent parts for debugging.

faqs.a0: The origin is the combination of protocol, domain, and port.
faqs.a1: Everything after the '?' is split into key-value pairs separated by '&'.

url-parser.sidebar_desc: This tool uses the standard URL API to reliably parse strings into components.
